title: Rebound

jokes.text: David, when did you become the Grim Reaper of romance?
jokes.setup: David expressing concern about Alexis's rebound relationship
jokes.punchline: Comparing David's pessimism to the Grim Reaper
jokes.explanation: Sharp metaphor that captures David's character while being economical and visual

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm just saying, she's clearly not thinking straight. She's vulnerable right now, and this guy is taking advantage of that.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: David, you're being a little dark about this, don't you think?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm being realistic.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You're being the Grim Reaper. You walk into a room and flowers wilt.

jokes.text: We were just having the most stimulating conversation about you... ganda! Ugandan... politics.
jokes.setup: Being caught talking about Alexis when she arrives
jokes.punchline: Moira's terrible attempt to cover by extending 'you' to 'Uganda'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira moment - her verbose nature creates an absurd cover story that's obviously false

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: We were just talking about... you... ganda. Uganda. The country.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Uganda?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Yes, it's a fascinating place. We were discussing its natural resources and political climate.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Mmhmm, very pressing issues in Uganda.

jokes.text: Were you guys waiting up for me? - Ah! - N-no!
jokes.setup: Alexis catches them obviously talking about her
jokes.punchline: Their panicked, unconvincing denials
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom beat but executed well with the family dynamic

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: What were you guys just talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Nothing! We were just... talking about the weather.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, very boring weather talk.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You were literally just looking at me.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: No, we weren't.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: We were looking... past you. At the wall.

jokes.text: it was like, way too expensive, but hey, I'm not the one paying
jokes.setup: Describing her date at an expensive steakhouse
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting she expects men to pay
jokes.explanation: Classic Alexis entitlement delivered matter-of-factly

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I mean, he took me to this incredible steakhouse, and I just... I assumed he was going to pay. Like, obviously.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I don't know, it's just always been that way for me. Men pay. That's just how it works.

jokes.text: I say, bravo, Alexis. Fling forward!
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining she's putting herself out there after Ted
jokes.punchline: Moira's theatrical encouragement with a play on words
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira wordplay combining theatrical support with alliteration

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, that's the spirit! You need to get back out there and show the world what you're made of. You're a catch, darling. A real catch. So go forth and... reel them in.

jokes.text: but you can call me Davey. Hey, how you doin', Davey?
jokes.setup: David sarcastically offering a nickname
jokes.punchline: Artie immediately using it back on David
jokes.explanation: David's sarcasm backfires as Artie completely misses it and turns it around

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh, well, if you need a nickname, I could call you 'Art.' You know, short for Artie.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Oh, that's great, Dave. Real creative. Hey, speaking of nicknames, how about I call you 'Clueless'? Short for David.

jokes.text: Our Lexi is making some wonderful choices.
jokes.setup: Just met Artie who calls Alexis 'Lexi' and seems inappropriate
jokes.punchline: Moira's deadpan sarcastic assessment
jokes.explanation: Moira's perfectly timed sarcasm about an obviously poor choice

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, he certainly seems like a man who respects personal boundaries and the concept of consent.

jokes.text: Like you calling the florist, and getting a quote? - That might be one of them.
jokes.setup: Patrick vaguely explaining variables preventing a floral quote
jokes.punchline: David pointing out the obvious first step Patrick hasn't done
jokes.explanation: Good relationship comedy showing Patrick's procrastination and David's directness

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Well, there are a lot of variables that could affect the final quote.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Have you asked them what they want?

jokes.text: Jocelyn has been smiling and waving at me from across the room in a very disturbing way
jokes.setup: David noticing Jocelyn's behavior
jokes.punchline: Describing normal friendliness as 'disturbing'
jokes.explanation: Perfect David interpretation of normal social interaction as threatening

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Why is she being so nice to me? It's actually quite disturbing.

jokes.text: I'll be the mother with the baby at home looking for some extra cash, after I invested in your father's business.
jokes.setup: Casual job inquiry at the store
jokes.punchline: Escalating to emotional manipulation and guilt
jokes.explanation: Jocelyn's manipulation escalates quickly from simple job request to guilt trip

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: So I was just wondering if you might have any positions available at the store?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, we're not really hiring right now.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, I really need a job. My family's going through some financial difficulties.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm sorry to hear that, but—

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: My mother's sick. And my father left us. I don't have anywhere else to turn, David. You're my only hope.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I understand, but I really can't—

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: I guess some people just don't care about the suffering of others.

jokes.text: There is an opening for a waitress at Bazongas Gentlemen's Club. I guess I could try that.
jokes.setup: Looking for employment alternatives
jokes.punchline: Casually suggesting working at a strip club
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation delivered matter-of-factly, with great club name

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Have you considered working at a strip club? The tips are really good, and you get to meet so many interesting people.

jokes.text: I don't know what's wrong with Bazongas.
jokes.setup: Patrick and David's horrified reaction to strip club suggestion
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's innocent defense of the establishment
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of an absurd defense, very quotable

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: It's a very clean establishment. They have a strict no-touching policy.

jokes.text: More casual?
jokes.setup: Jocelyn saying she needs to change into something more casual
jokes.punchline: David's incredulous reaction to her already very casual appearance
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy about Jocelyn's fashion sense that we can infer from David's reaction

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, what? You're already wearing... that.

jokes.text: When you have that many years under your belt, you're bound to have collected a good story or two.
jokes.setup: Alexis saying Artie has good stories
jokes.punchline: Johnny's passive-aggressive comment about Artie's age
jokes.explanation: Subtle dig at the age gap disguised as innocent observation

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, he would have good stories. He's been alive for quite some time.

jokes.text: Artie loves to scat along with the songs. - Do you know what scatting is? - I do, not a huge fan.
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining Artie's jazz enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Johnny's understated disapproval
jokes.explanation: Good timing and character consistency in Johnny's polite dismissal

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Artie is really into jazz. Like, really into it.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Mm-hmm.

jokes.text: Artie's an old soul, and you know how I'm an old soul, so we're just like, two old souls having fun together. - Well, he is an old soul. I'll give you that.
jokes.setup: Alexis describing their spiritual compatibility
jokes.punchline: Johnny's literal interpretation about Artie's actual age
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay where Johnny takes 'old soul' literally, creating perfect ironic commentary

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: We're spiritually compatible. He's like, an old soul.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, he's 73.

jokes.text: his ex-wife owns a vineyard, and his kids and grandkids are gonna be there.
jokes.setup: Describing a casual weekend trip
jokes.punchline: Revealing it involves meeting his entire family including grandchildren
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how un-casual this 'casual' relationship is

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: So I'm thinking of taking a casual weekend trip.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That sounds nice. Where are you going?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, it's to meet someone's family.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh my God, how serious is this?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Very serious. I'm meeting his entire family, including his grandchildren.

jokes.text: Oh my God, is he calling me right now?! That is so 2001.
jokes.setup: Phone ringing during their conversation
jokes.punchline: Alexis's horror at receiving an actual phone call
jokes.explanation: Perfect generational humor and character moment - very quotable

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, is someone actually calling me? Like, on the phone? Like a psychopath?

jokes.text: Yep, you just double-tap on photo with your finger.
jokes.setup: Taking a call from her much older boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Having to explain basic phone functions to him
jokes.explanation: Good generational gap humor showing the relationship's problems

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: No, you don't need to yell into the phone. Yes, I can hear you. No, the phone doesn't need to be plugged in. Well, it does eventually, but not right now.

jokes.text: speaking as someone who uses the body milk on the regs, just had a baby, don't wanna get stretch marks
jokes.setup: Professional customer service interaction
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn oversharing intimate personal details
jokes.explanation: Classic Jocelyn oversharing in inappropriate professional context

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Hi! Welcome! Can I help you find anything today?

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: Yes, I'm looking for something in a medium.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Oh, a medium! That's actually the size my gynecologist said my uterus is. Medium-sized. I have a tilted uterus, which is why I can't use tampons, so I have to use pads, and let me tell you, the chafing in the summer is just—well, it's unbearable.

jokes.text: plus it's great for diaper rash
jokes.setup: Already inappropriate sales pitch about body products
jokes.punchline: Adding even more inappropriate uses
jokes.explanation: Escalation of the inappropriate sales technique

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: You can also use it as a personal lubricant, a hair gel, or a cooking oil.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, what?

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: It's very versatile.

jokes.text: You guys make it look so much harder.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn making her first sale easily
jokes.punchline: Insulting David and Patrick's professional competence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jocelyn's unconscious insensitivity

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: You know, I've been thinking about getting into real estate, and I just sold a house in like five minutes.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's great, Jocelyn.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Yeah, it really makes me wonder how you and Patrick have been in this business for so long and haven't figured it out yet.

jokes.text: Have a 'Rose-y' day! - We won't be doing 'Rose-y day.' - No?
jokes.setup: Jocelyn creating a cheerful catchphrase for the store
jokes.punchline: David's immediate veto and Patrick's diplomatic response
jokes.explanation: Perfect David reaction to forced cheerfulness, sets up callback

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: How about 'Rose Apothecary: We're here to help!'

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: That's... that's actually pretty good, Jocelyn.

jokes.text: Either a flock of poultry has delivered its ova mid-flight upon our car, or I've become the victim of a vandalization!
jokes.setup: Discovering eggs on their car
jokes.punchline: Moira's characteristically verbose description of being egged
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moira vocabulary - 'ova mid-flight' is peak pretentious description of eggs, highly quotable

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I've been assailed. Positively besieged by what appears to be avian projectiles. My car looks like it's been through some sort of poultry-based apocalypse. And I smell like a breakfast buffet that's been left out in the sun.

jokes.text: I can think of a handful of people who'd wanna egg David.
jokes.setup: Moira assuming the egging was targeted at her
jokes.punchline: Stevie's deadpan suggestion it might be about David instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan delivery that deflates Moira's drama while roasting David

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Or maybe it's about David.

jokes.text: Stevie, you are blessed with anonymity, and thus will never have to know the crippling fear that accompanies global repute.
jokes.setup: Stevie suggesting it might not be about Moira
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic description of fame's burdens
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira self-importance delivered with theatrical flair, very quotable

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, it is always about me, David. I am a woman of a certain notoriety. When I walk down the street, people point and stare. I cannot simply pop into a grocery store without causing a minor disturbance. Fame is a burden, darling, and one that I have carried with grace and poise for decades.

jokes.text: After my first centerfold with Soap Opera Digest, a deranged fan deposited locks of hair into my glove compartment.
jokes.setup: Explaining the dangers of fame
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous story about soap opera magazine 'fame'
jokes.explanation: Absurd and specific backstory that's perfectly Moira - soap opera 'centerfold' is brilliantly ridiculous

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You know, fame is a very dangerous thing. I once knew a woman who appeared on the cover of a soap opera digest, and within weeks, she was recognized at a Piggly Wiggly in Ohio. The stress was unbearable.

jokes.text: As of now, you are my star witness. And sit low, no point endangering both our lives.
jokes.setup: Treating car egging as serious crime
jokes.punchline: Dramatic precautions as if they're in mortal danger
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Moira's paranoia to action movie levels

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: We need to take every precaution. Board up the windows, install motion sensors, and for God's sake, someone get me a suit of armor.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Moira, it's just egg.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Just egg? JUST EGG? Do you know what egg does to a finish, David?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Ruins it?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Ruins it. We're essentially under siege.

jokes.text: Stevie, you are blessed with anonymity, and thus will never have to know the crippling fear that accompanies global repute.
jokes.setup: Stevie suggests David might be the target
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic speech about the burden of fame in response to simple observation
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira pomposity. 'Blessed with anonymity' and 'crippling fear of global repute' is perfectly over-the-top.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, of course he's the target. Do you know what it's like to be married to someone of my stature? The constant scrutiny, the relentless attention, the burden of being photographed at one's worst angles. I didn't ask for this life of notoriety, and yet here I am, bearing the weight of recognition with nothing but my grace and my bone structure to sustain me.

jokes.text: and she puts thing, says it's a lunner menu. - Lunner!
jokes.setup: Artie telling a restaurant story
jokes.punchline: The made-up word 'lunner' for lunch/dinner
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that shows the generational gap through language

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: So I'm at this restaurant, right? And the host says, 'We don't serve lunch or dinner, we serve lunner.' I said, 'What the hell is lunner?'

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: That's not a real word.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: That's what I said! But apparently it's the meal between lunch and dinner, so it's lunner.

jokes.text: Oh don't worry, I'm over 21, Dad.
jokes.setup: Johnny commenting about mimosas with concern about Artie
jokes.punchline: Alexis missing the point about age appropriateness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alexis completely missing her dad's concern

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Alexis, you can't be drinking mimosas at this hour. What will people think?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Dad, mimosas are literally just orange juice. It's basically breakfast.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: That's not the point. It's about propriety and—

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, you're right. I should wait until it's a more appropriate time, like noon.

jokes.text: Well, please don't call me 'Dad.'
jokes.setup: Artie asking to be called 'Artie' and calling Johnny 'Dad'
jokes.punchline: Johnny's understated horror at the implication
jokes.explanation: Perfect understated reaction to the uncomfortable family dynamic implications

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm not your dad.

jokes.text: I don't know, I was a baby at the time, but there's a photo of him holding me at Christmas. And I remember that, because that was the year Santa brought me a lighter.
jokes.setup: Explaining how she might know Artie from her past
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of receiving a lighter as a baby from Santa
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Twyla backstory detail - baby getting a lighter from Santa is perfectly dark and memorable

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Well, Santa gave me a lighter when I was a baby.

jokes.text: That's a smidge worse than 'Dad.'
jokes.setup: Artie calling him 'old man' instead of Dad
jokes.punchline: Johnny's precise assessment of the insult escalation
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the uncomfortable naming situation

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, that's not an insult, that's just a factual statement about my age.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: But if you're going to call me 'old man' instead of 'Dad,' then I'm going to start calling you 'disappointment' instead of 'son.'

jokes.text: I own a beautiful three-bedroom home with an above ground pool, if that's what you're worried about.
jokes.setup: Johnny asking about his occupation
jokes.punchline: Artie's proud description of modest middle-class lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Good class comedy showing different perspectives on what constitutes success

dialogue.speaker: Johnny Rose
dialogue.line: What do you do, Artie?

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Well, I'm a semi-retired textile importer. I own a modest home in the suburbs, drive a sensible sedan, and I take my family on one nice vacation every other year.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I'd say I'm living the dream.

jokes.text: And being a grandad's even harder. - Oh, well, maybe you might wanna give me some tips when I eventually get there.
jokes.setup: Artie offering parenting wisdom
jokes.punchline: Johnny's pointed suggestion that Artie might become a grandfather through Alexis
jokes.explanation: Dark and clever comeback that makes the age gap implications explicit

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You know, being a parent is about guiding your children, showing them the way...

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes, well, perhaps you should focus on that before Alexis makes you a grandfather.

jokes.text: You describe the town as, and I quote, 'the last place you'd ever wanna end up.' - Oh no, I described the town as the last place I'd ever want to end up.
jokes.setup: Roland quoting Moira's insulting magazine comment
jokes.punchline: Moira's meaningless correction of the exact wording
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moira moment - she thinks correcting 'wanna' to 'want to' somehow changes the meaning

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You said, 'Roland Schitt is a man of very limited means and even more limited faculties.'

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I said 'faculties,' not 'facilities.' There's a difference.

jokes.text: this could be the biggest scandal since we installed the church wheelchair ramp backwards
jokes.setup: Describing the severity of the PR crisis
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to an absurdly minor previous 'scandal'
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd small-town reference that's both specific and ridiculous

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: This PR crisis is the worst thing that's ever happened to this town.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: It makes the time I wore the same suit two days in a row look like nothing.

jokes.text: we can't afford another 'ramp-gate'
jokes.setup: Referencing the wheelchair ramp scandal
jokes.punchline: Turning it into a '-gate' scandal name
jokes.explanation: Good callback with political scandal naming convention applied to small-town mishap

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You know, I've been thinking about this wheelchair ramp situation, and I think we should call it 'Rampgate.'

jokes.text: I specifically commended your ever-beating heart, and your elephantine generosity.
jokes.setup: Moira trying to defend her magazine comments
jokes.punchline: Her characteristically overwrought compliments that sound condescending
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira vocabulary where 'elephantine' sounds like an insult despite good intentions

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I was simply being complimentary. I said she had wonderful bone structure for someone of her... stature.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: And I meant it as a genuine compliment that she managed to maintain such a... trim silhouette despite her obvious love of carbohydrates.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I was merely pointing out that for someone with her particular genetic disadvantages, she presented herself quite admirably.

jokes.text: Please know that I believe, deep down, there are worse places to be. Like in your shoes right now.
jokes.setup: Attempting to apologize and make amends
jokes.punchline: Accidentally insulting the townspeople while trying to compliment the town
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly backfired apology where Moira accidentally makes it worse

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I want to apologize to this town. You've all been so welcoming, and I realize I haven't always been... gracious in return.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: This town may not have the sophistication of where I come from, but it has a certain... rustic charm. Like a quaint little dollhouse, if the dollhouse smelled like manure and desperation.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: And you people! So simple, yet somehow managing to function despite your obvious limitations. It's really quite inspiring.

jokes.text: As soon as one of you details my car.
jokes.setup: Making a solemn vow to make things right
jokes.punchline: Immediately demanding the townspeople clean her car
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira entitlement that completely undermines her previous apology

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I make a solemn vow to make things right with this town.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Now, who among you will clean my car?

jokes.text: like when you walk into a casino and win big on your first pull of the 'Basic Instinct' slot machine
jokes.setup: Explaining Jocelyn's beginner's luck in sales
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific reference to a movie-themed slot machine
jokes.explanation: Weird and specific David reference that reveals his gambling experience

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: She's experiencing what I like to call 'beginner's luck.' It's a very real phenomenon.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I think it's wonderful. She's a natural.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Or she's just won the equivalent of pulling the lever on a Casablanca slot machine.

jokes.text: Okay, I'm beginning to think that that is the high point of your entire life.
jokes.setup: David's oddly specific casino reference
jokes.punchline: Patrick's deadpan assessment of David's life experiences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Patrick response that roasts David while staying in character

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: You've been to a casino?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, Patrick. I've been to a casino.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: And you lost money at the blackjack table?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: While wearing a blazer that cost more than my car?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It was a very nice blazer, Patrick.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I'm not doubting the blazer. I'm just trying to figure out how you managed to have money to lose.

jokes.text: His taste in makeout music, for example, like, so weird. Like, who even is Cat Stevens?!
jokes.setup: Alexis listing Artie's flaws after being dumped
jokes.punchline: Not knowing a famous musician from before her time
jokes.explanation: Good generational gap humor showing Alexis's limited cultural knowledge

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: He didn't know who Cher was.

jokes.text: Well, Cat wrote some wonderful songs, honey.
jokes.setup: Alexis dismissing Cat Stevens
jokes.punchline: Johnny's gentle defense of the classic musician
jokes.explanation: Sweet father moment trying to bridge the generation gap

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Cat Stevens? More like Cat has-beens.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, actually, Cat Stevens is a very talented musician. He had some wonderful hits in the '70s.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Dad, nobody cares about Cat Stevens.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: That's not true. Morning Has Broken is a beautiful song.

jokes.text: He was responsible. He did run a successful mobile-home business.
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to defend Artie to Alexis
jokes.punchline: His attempts at positive spin on trailer parks
jokes.explanation: Good class comedy in Johnny's attempts to find the upside

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, they're very... efficient. You know, minimal square footage means minimal heating costs in the winter.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: And the sense of community is actually quite lovely. Everyone knows their neighbors.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Plus, there's a certain rustic charm to the open-air living concept.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: And the trailer itself is mobile, which is really quite innovative when you think about it.

jokes.text: Where were you when I was dating half the cast of 'White Squall?'
jokes.setup: Johnny saying he should have protected her more
jokes.punchline: Alexis's casual reference to dating multiple actors from one movie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis backstory reference that's both absurd and character-specific, highly quotable

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I should have protected you more.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Dad, I dated like half the cast of 'Fast Five.'

jokes.text: I just don't think it's in my brand. - I don't think I've ever said that.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn rejecting the job using David-like language
jokes.punchline: David's confused denial of his own typical phrasing
jokes.explanation: Great reversal where Jocelyn uses David's pretentious language against him

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: I appreciate the offer, but it's just not my aesthetic, and I'm not going to compromise my integrity for a position that doesn't align with my values.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, what? Did you just... No. I don't talk like that.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: You literally just said something very similar yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, yes, but when I say it, it's different.

jokes.text: 'Cause you might need it a little more than me.
jokes.setup: Offering to let them keep the sales bell
jokes.punchline: Implying they need help with sales more than she does
jokes.explanation: Perfect final insult delivered with fake kindness

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: You can keep the sales bell. I don't really need it anyway.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: I figured you two could probably use it more than I do.

jokes.text: I'll have the Miso Black Cod, please. - We don't have that. - You mean, you're sold out, is more like it!
jokes.setup: Moira ordering upscale food at the local café
jokes.punchline: Refusing to accept they don't serve fancy food
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira delusion about the town's amenities

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Do you have any quail? Or perhaps some foie gras?

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: We have tuna salad.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Tuna salad? Twyla, I don't eat anything that swims in a can.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: It's fresh tuna.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, do you have anything with edible gold leaf? Or at least some microgreens?

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: We have iceberg lettuce.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Iceberg? That's not even a real vegetable, it's a water delivery system. I'll just have a sparkling water. Make sure it's imported.

jokes.text: You mean, you're sold out, is more like it!
jokes.setup: Restaurant not having what Moira ordered for tourism video
jokes.punchline: Her attempt to spin their lack of menu items as popularity
jokes.explanation: Good Moira damage control attempt. Her desperate spin on the restaurant's limitations for the tourism video shows her promotional instincts.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, they're clearly so popular, they've run out of everything. It's a testament to their success. We should film that.

jokes.text: No noisy customers or busy cash registers here, to intrude upon your inner cogitations. - It's-it's not usually this quiet. - Oh, it's always nice and quiet here.
jokes.setup: Moira filming tourism ad about peaceful apothecary
jokes.punchline: Moira insisting the store is always empty despite David's protests
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where Moira doubles down on insulting David's business

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: This apothecary is a peaceful sanctuary, where one can browse in complete and utter solitude.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, there are literally customers here right now.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David, please. I'm trying to convey an atmosphere of zen-like emptiness.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: But it's not empty. There are people shopping.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, they're not supposed to be here during filming.

jokes.text: Can you stop saying that?
jokes.setup: Moira repeatedly emphasizing how quiet/empty the store is
jokes.punchline: David's desperate plea to stop highlighting their lack of business
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat. David's panic at his mother's 'helpful' promotion perfectly captures the family dynamic and business concerns.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Can you please stop saying that? Every time you say it, a customer doesn't come in.

jokes.text: Thank you for having us. - John, no.
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to be polite during the tourism video
jokes.punchline: Moira immediately shutting down his attempted participation
jokes.explanation: Perfect married couple moment with maximum economy

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, I think what makes Schitt's Creek special is the sense of community and the genuine warmth of the people who live here.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: If heaven had a creek, it would be this one.
jokes.setup: Concluding the tourism video with a slogan
jokes.punchline: Moira's overwrought play on the town name
jokes.explanation: Typical Moira attempt at profundity that sounds more pretentious than inspiring

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Schitt's Creek: where the name may disappoint, but the experience won't.

tmdb_overview: Johnny becomes overly involved in Alexis' romantic life, while Moira offends the townspeople in an interview with People magazine.
insight: David's deadpan reactions carry an 83-point episode built on character timing alone.
